visualAssessment.before: Plain geometric primitives (icosahedron, octahedron, torus-knot), flat opaque materials, barely-changing scrub between sections — user complaint: stuck in one place.
visualAssessment.after: Iter3 screenshots show a layered Core with iridescent clearcoat mid-shell, bright inner sphere, wireframe HUD cage, 96 orbiting instanced particles and additive lightning segments; each module is a themed device (Frontend holo-slab with scanlines, Backend stacked racks with LEDs, DevOps twin-tori + orbiting pills, Cloud multi-sphere cluster with volumetric aura, Mobile phone slab with orbiting satellite); ground reflection is faintly visible; Sparkles add data-dust depth; bloom is richer. Each section now has a distinct pinned 3D state visible in the screenshots: Hero shows the core assembling mid-pin, About shows a zoomed intimate framing with dimmed modules behind the glass panel, Skills shows the Frontend module focused with core+cloud+devops visible around it, Projects shows modules lined up horizontally in front of a lifted core, Experience shows an orbital camera view with dimmed modules, Contact shows modules contracted inward toward an intensely glowing collapsed core. Transition SMASH uses ChromaticAberration via a store-driven controller inside the EffectComposer. Remaining gap: Hero screenshot still partially shows the core because the test lands mid-pin rather than at start — expected since the pin scrubs the assembly animation; actual user scroll will see the full fade-in.

warnings: ScrollTriggers for About/Projects/Experience/Contact use pin:true; earlier Skills pin on inner div was preserved (500vh + sticky) because its 5 sub-step camera focus depends on fractional scroll over a longer range.
ChromaticAberration uses a small base offset (0.0006) and pulses to ~0.004 during section boundaries via sceneStore.fx.chromaticIntensity.
GroundReflector uses drei MeshReflectorMaterial at resolution 512 with heavy blur — kept conservative for perf; disable if mobile regresses.
